versioning.compatibility_policy: A consumer repo must record the governance package git SHA it deploys. Release tags improve readability and rollback communication, but tags do not replace the SHA record.

basis: Downstream e2e proof completed under issue #288 and mirrored by issue #329.
releases.consumer_guidance: Use this tag as the human-readable release coordinate, keep `0.1.0-contract` as the package version for this contract-era release, and keep the exact resolved git SHA in `.hldpro/governance-tooling.json`.

principles: CI remains authoritative; local gates are upstream filters.
Governed repos consume pinned tooling instead of copying shared logic by hand.
Repo-local overrides must be explicit, reviewable, and visible in the consumer record.
Managed files must be identifiable and reversible.
Issue #288 proved downstream pull, deploy, local enforcement, GitHub enforcement, and rollback or uninstall behavior end to end before the first release tag.

surfaces.notes: Runner logic is shared. Consumer repos should not fork this logic.

surfaces.version_rule: pin with runner by governance git SHA
surfaces.notes: Profiles encode repo-specific command mappings while keeping runner behavior shared.

surfaces.version_rule: shim records governance ref supplied at deploy time
surfaces.notes: Managed shims must include the managed marker and must not overwrite unmanaged files without explicit backup or force.
Package-level deployer writes the consumer record and delegates Local CI shim rendering to the compatibility deployer.

surfaces.version_rule: pin by governance git SHA when consumed outside governance
surfaces.notes: Active parallel roots warn only when declared; undeclared dirty roots still block.

surfaces.version_rule: pin by governance git SHA when used by governed repos
surfaces.notes: Governance-surface changes require issue-backed structured planning.
Inventory distinguishes local commands, workflow contracts, script dry-runs, and GitHub-only surfaces.

surfaces.version_rule: pin by governance git SHA for helper changes; generated outputs are committed as scoped artifacts
surfaces.notes: Scoped graph/wiki outputs are tracked baselines. Cache and per-run scratch files remain local-only.

artifact_classes.package_core: Shared logic owned by hldpro-governance. Consumers reference or deploy it from a pinned governance version.
artifact_classes.repo_profile: Repo-specific command mapping owned centrally but selected per consumer.
artifact_classes.managed_file: A file written into a consumer repo by a deployer and marked as managed.
artifact_classes.repo_local_override: Consumer-owned deviation that must be recorded in the consumer manifest and reviewed in that repo.
artifact_classes.tracked_baseline: Committed measurement or generated artifact used as evidence.
artifact_classes.per_run_report: Local or CI run output that is ignored unless summarized into a closeout or issue comment.

managed_file_contract.overwrite_policy: Refuse unmanaged files by default; require backup or force with issue evidence.
managed_file_contract.rollback_policy: Managed-file rollback must either restore the previous managed version, remove the managed file, or restore an explicit backup. Phase 2 deployer must expose dry-run rollback preview before apply.

forbidden: Forking package core logic without an issue-backed exception.
Disabling CI-required gates through local package configuration.
Treating a dry-run as live enforcement evidence.

phase_2_deployer_requires: Support dry-run, apply, verify, and rollback modes.
Write or update the consumer record.
Refuse dirty target repos unless the execution scope explicitly allows the active lane.
Refuse unmanaged file overwrite by default.
phase_gate.phase_4_pilot_fallback_rule: A replacement repo may be selected only if the Phase 4 issue proves local-ai-machine cannot create a clean adoption worktree or cannot run package deployment prerequisites.
